Core components
1. Section-first plan schema
# phoenix_v4/planning/schema_v4.py

@dataclass
class SectionPlan:
    """A section within a chapter."""
    section: int
    type: str  # HOOK, TEACHING, SCENE, STORY, EXERCISE, REFLECTION, INTEGRATION
    purpose: str
    variants: List[VariantPlan]  # ≥3 variants (mandatory)
    story_eligible: bool
    story_layer: Optional[str]  # L1, L2, L3 if story_eligible
    location_aware: bool
    location_class: Optional[str]  # urban_east_coast, suburban_midwest, etc.

@dataclass
class VariantPlan:
    """A single variant of a section."""
    variant_id: str
    content_id: str  # Points to section template library
    scene_id: Optional[str]  # If type=SCENE
    location_profile: Optional[str]  # If location_aware=True

@dataclass
class ChapterPlanV4:
    """A chapter in V4 (section-first)."""
    chapter: int
    title: str
    sections: List[SectionPlan]  # 8-14 sections per chapter
    story_plan: Optional[StoryPlan]  # Governed story selection

@dataclass
class BookPlanV4:
    """Complete V4 book plan."""
    book_id: str
    topic: str
    persona: str
    character_id: str
    location: Optional[str]
    book_seed: int  # For deterministic variant selection
    chapters: List[ChapterPlanV4]  # 12-20+ chapters
# phoenix_v4/planning/schema_v4.py@dataclassclass SectionPlan:    """A section within a chapter."""    section: int    type: str  # HOOK, TEACHING, SCENE, STORY, EXERCISE, REFLECTION, INTEGRATION    purpose: str    variants: List[VariantPlan]  # ≥3 variants (mandatory)    story_eligible: bool    story_layer: Optional[str]  # L1, L2, L3 if story_eligible    location_aware: bool    location_class: Optional[str]  # urban_east_coast, suburban_midwest, etc.@dataclassclass VariantPlan:    """A single variant of a section."""    variant_id: str    content_id: str  # Points to section template library    scene_id: Optional[str]  # If type=SCENE    location_profile: Optional[str]  # If location_aware=True@dataclassclass ChapterPlanV4:    """A chapter in V4 (section-first)."""    chapter: int    title: str    sections: List[SectionPlan]  # 8-14 sections per chapter    story_plan: Optional[StoryPlan]  # Governed story selection@dataclassclass BookPlanV4:    """Complete V4 book plan."""    book_id: str    topic: str    persona: str    character_id: str    location: Optional[str]    book_seed: int  # For deterministic variant selection    chapters: List[ChapterPlanV4]  # 12-20+ chapters
2. Variant coverage validator
# phoenix_v4/validator/variant_validator.py

class VariantValidator:
    """Enforces mandatory variant coverage."""
    
    def validate_variant_coverage(self, plan: BookPlanV4) -> List[ValidationError]:
        """
        Ensures every section has ≥3 variants.
        High-risk sections (HOOK, SCENE, INTEGRATION) must have 5-7.
        """
        errors = []
        
        for chapter in plan.chapters:
            for section in chapter.sections:
                variant_count = len(section.variants)
                
                if section.type in ["HOOK", "SCENE", "INTEGRATION"]:
                    if variant_count < 5:
                        errors.append(ValidationError(
                            field=f"chapters[{chapter.chapter}].sections[{section.section}]",
                            message=f"High-risk section {section.type} requires ≥5 variants, found {variant_count}",
                            severity="error"
                        ))
                else:
                    if variant_count < 3:
                        errors.append(ValidationError(
                            field=f"chapters[{chapter.chapter}].sections[{section.section}]",
                            message=f"Section {section.type} requires ≥3 variants, found {variant_count}",
                            severity="error"
                        ))
        
        return errors
# phoenix_v4/validator/variant_validator.pyclass VariantValidator:    """Enforces mandatory variant coverage."""        def validate_variant_coverage(self, plan: BookPlanV4) -> List[ValidationError]:        """        Ensures every section has ≥3 variants.        High-risk sections (HOOK, SCENE, INTEGRATION) must have 5-7.        """        errors = []                for chapter in plan.chapters:            for section in chapter.sections:                variant_count = len(section.variants)                                if section.type in ["HOOK", "SCENE", "INTEGRATION"]:                    if variant_count < 5:                        errors.append(ValidationError(                            field=f"chapters[{chapter.chapter}].sections[{section.section}]",                            message=f"High-risk section {section.type} requires ≥5 variants, found {variant_count}",                            severity="error"                        ))                else:                    if variant_count < 3:                        errors.append(ValidationError(                            field=f"chapters[{chapter.chapter}].sections[{section.section}]",                            message=f"Section {section.type} requires ≥3 variants, found {variant_count}",                            severity="error"                        ))                return errors
3. Section-first assembler
# phoenix_v4/assembler/assemble_v4.py

class AssemblerV4:
    """V4 section-first assembler."""
    
    def assemble_chapter(self, chapter_plan: ChapterPlanV4, book_plan: BookPlanV4) -> str:
        """
        Assemble chapter using section-first approach.
        """
        sections_content = []
        
        for section_plan in chapter_plan.sections:
            # Select variant deterministically based on book_seed
            variant = self._select_variant(section_plan, book_plan.book_seed)
            
            # Assemble section content
            section_content = self._assemble_section(section_plan, variant, book_plan)
            
            sections_content.append(section_content)
        
        return "\n\n".join(sections_content)
    
    def _select_variant(self, section_plan: SectionPlan, book_seed: int) -> VariantPlan:
        """
        Deterministic variant selection based on book_seed.
        Same seed → same variant selection.
        """
        # Use hash of (book_seed, chapter_num, section_num) to select variant
        hash_value = hash((book_seed, section_plan.section))
        variant_index = hash_value % len(section_plan.variants)
        return section_plan.variants[variant_index]
    
    def _assemble_section(self, section_plan: SectionPlan, variant: VariantPlan, book_plan: BookPlanV4) -> str:
        """
        Assemble a single section.
        """
        # Load section template
        content = self.libraries.load_section(variant.content_id)
        
        # If scene, load location-aware scene
        if section_plan.type == "SCENE" and variant.scene_id:
            scene_content = self.libraries.load_scene_v4(
                variant.scene_id,
                book_plan.persona,
                book_plan.topic,
                variant.location_profile
            )
            content = self._inject_scene(content, scene_content)
        
        # If story-eligible and story present, inject story
        if section_plan.story_eligible and chapter_plan.story_plan:
            story_content = self.libraries.load_story_v4(
                chapter_plan.story_plan.story_id,
                chapter_plan.story_plan.layer
            )
            content = self._inject_story(content, story_content)
        
        # Character substitution
        content = self._substitute_character(content, book_plan.character_id)
        
        # Location hydration (if location_aware)
        if section_plan.location_aware:
            content = self.location_hydrator.hydrate(content, variant.location_profile)
        
        return content
# phoenix_v4/assembler/assemble_v4.pyclass AssemblerV4:    """V4 section-first assembler."""        def assemble_chapter(self, chapter_plan: ChapterPlanV4, book_plan: BookPlanV4) -> str:        """        Assemble chapter using section-first approach.        """        sections_content = []                for section_plan in chapter_plan.sections:            # Select variant deterministically based on book_seed            variant = self._select_variant(section_plan, book_plan.book_seed)                        # Assemble section content            section_content = self._assemble_section(section_plan, variant, book_plan)                        sections_content.append(section_content)                return "\n\n".join(sections_content)        def _select_variant(self, section_plan: SectionPlan, book_seed: int) -> VariantPlan:        """        Deterministic variant selection based on book_seed.        Same seed → same variant selection.        """        # Use hash of (book_seed, chapter_num, section_num) to select variant        hash_value = hash((book_seed, section_plan.section))        variant_index = hash_value % len(section_plan.variants)        return section_plan.variants[variant_index]        def _assemble_section(self, section_plan: SectionPlan, variant: VariantPlan, book_plan: BookPlanV4) -> str:        """        Assemble a single section.        """        # Load section template        content = self.libraries.load_section(variant.content_id)                # If scene, load location-aware scene        if section_plan.type == "SCENE" and variant.scene_id:            scene_content = self.libraries.load_scene_v4(                variant.scene_id,                book_plan.persona,                book_plan.topic,                variant.location_profile            )            content = self._inject_scene(content, scene_content)                # If story-eligible and story present, inject story        if section_plan.story_eligible and chapter_plan.story_plan:            story_content = self.libraries.load_story_v4(                chapter_plan.story_plan.story_id,                chapter_plan.story_plan.layer            )            content = self._inject_story(content, story_content)                # Character substitution        content = self._substitute_character(content, book_plan.character_id)                # Location hydration (if location_aware)        if section_plan.location_aware:            content = self.location_hydrator.hydrate(content, variant.location_profile)                return content
4. Story governance (V4)
# phoenix_v4/policies/story_policy_v4.yaml

story_governance:
  # Per-chapter limits
  max_stories_per_chapter: 2  # More than V3 (1)
  
  # Section-level eligibility
  section_eligibility:
    HOOK:
      story_eligible: false
    TEACHING:
      story_eligible: true
      allowed_layers: [L1]  # Recognition only
    SCENE:
      story_eligible: false
    STORY:
      story_eligible: true
      allowed_layers: [L1, L2, L3]
    REFLECTION:
      story_eligible: true
      allowed_layers: [L2, L3]  # Effort/Return
    INTEGRATION:
      story_eligible: true
      allowed_layers: [L3]  # Return only
  
  # Uniqueness
  uniqueness:
    within_book: true  # No story_id reuse within book
    cross_book_reuse: "variant_framing"  # Allowed with variant framing
  
  # Density
  min_stories_per_book: 8  # Stories are core structural layer
  max_stories_per_book: 24  # Cap to prevent overuse
# phoenix_v4/policies/story_policy_v4.yamlstory_governance:  # Per-chapter limits  max_stories_per_chapter: 2  # More than V3 (1)    # Section-level eligibility  section_eligibility:    HOOK:      story_eligible: false    TEACHING:      story_eligible: true      allowed_layers: [L1]  # Recognition only    SCENE:      story_eligible: false    STORY:      story_eligible: true      allowed_layers: [L1, L2, L3]    REFLECTION:      story_eligible: true      allowed_layers: [L2, L3]  # Effort/Return    INTEGRATION:      story_eligible: true      allowed_layers: [L3]  # Return only    # Uniqueness  uniqueness:    within_book: true  # No story_id reuse within book    cross_book_reuse: "variant_framing"  # Allowed with variant framing    # Density  min_stories_per_book: 8  # Stories are core structural layer  max_stories_per_book: 24  # Cap to prevent overuse
Implementation plan
Phase 1: Foundation (Week 1)
Create phoenix_v4/ directory structure
Implement V4 plan schema (planning/schema_v4.py)
Create section type definitions (planning/section_types.py)
Set up shared library access (read-only from V3)
Phase 2: Validators (Week 1-2)
Implement validate_plan_v4.py (schema + structure)
Implement variant_validator.py (coverage enforcement)
Implement story_validator_v4.py (V4 story governance)
Implement section_validator.py (section structure)
Phase 3: Libraries (Week 2)
Implement loader_v4.py (V4 library loader)
Implement section_loader.py (section templates)
Implement variant_loader.py (variant content)
Implement scene_loader_v4.py (location-aware scenes)
Implement story_loader_v4.py (wraps shared story bank)
Phase 4: Assembler (Week 2-3)
Implement assemble_v4.py (main assembler)
Implement section_assembler.py (section-level logic)
Implement variant_selector.py (deterministic selection)
Implement location_hydrator_v4.py (location-aware)
Implement story_injector_v4.py (V4 story injection)
Phase 5: Policies (Week 3)
Create variant_policy.yaml (mandatory coverage)
Create story_policy_v4.yaml (long-form governance)
Create section_policy.yaml (section eligibility)
Create location_policy.yaml (location-aware rules)
Phase 6: Testing (Week 3-4)
Create example V4 plan (1 chapter, section-first)
Test validation (variant coverage, story governance)
Test assembly (section-first, variant selection)
Generate first V4 book
Principles (same as V3)
LLM = Planner (offline, once)
System = Assembler (deterministic, no decisions)
Frozen plan is the single source of truth
Hard-fail validation
Assembler is lookup-only
No runtime creativity
Auditability > cleverness
What V4 adds
Section-first planning (not chapter-first)
Mandatory variant coverage (anti-spam)
Governed story engine (core structural layer)
Location-aware scenes (persona + topic + location_class)
Multi-pass teaching (governed, no duplication)
Flexible chapter count (12-20+)
Long-form depth (40-80k+ words)
Next steps
Draft the V4 Planning Schema (detailed YAML structure)
Write the Content Team Writing Spec (V4 section templates)
Design Story Slot Policy v2 (long-form governance)
Create the directory structure and initial files
Which should I start with?

ChatGPT said:
Start with the Content Team Writing Spec (V4).
Here’s why, and how to sequence this cleanly without repeating past pain.

✅ What to start with (clear answer)
Start with: V4 Content Team Writing Spec (section-first)
Not schema. Not code. Not policies.
Writing spec first. Everything else becomes obvious once the content shape is locked.

Why this is the correct order (important)
You already have the hardest asset:
12 authored chapters that are actually good.
The failure mode before was:
Engineering guessing content structure
Content retrofitted into code
Duplication + variant confusion
V4 avoids this only if writing drives the system, not the other way around.

Correct build order for Phoenix Omega V4
Phase 0 — LOCK WRITING REALITY (now)
Deliverable: PHOENIX_OMEGA_V4_WRITING_SPEC.md
This answers:
What a section is
What types exist
How many variants per type
What may / may not repeat
How stories + scenes differ
How location is expressed in prose
How exercises progress across a long book
Until this exists, schema work is premature.

Phase 1 — One Chapter Proof (content-led)
Content team converts Chapter 1 only into:
8–12 sections
Correct section types
Required variant counts (5–7 where needed)
Location-aware scenes
Story-eligible flags (but no story writing yet)
This becomes the canonical example chapter.
If Chapter 1 works cleanly, the other 11 are mechanical.

Phase 2 — Plan Schema + Validator
Now engineering encodes:
Section-first schema (you already sketched this well)
Variant coverage rules
Story eligibility rules
At this point:
Validators reflect writing reality
Not imagined abstractions

Phase 3 — Assembler
Assembler is trivial once:
Sections are real
Variants exist
Locations are declared
Stories are selected, not invented

What the Writing Spec MUST contain (non-negotiable)
Your content team spec should explicitly define:
1. Section Types (V4 canonical set)
Example (final list can vary):
HOOK (5–7 variants)
SCENE (5–7 variants, location-bound)
TEACHING_PASS_1 (3–5 variants)
TEACHING_PASS_2 (3–5 variants)
REFLECTION (3–5 variants)
STORY_INTEGRATION (3–5 variants)
EXERCISE (brief | full rules)
INTEGRATION / TRANSITION (5 variants)
No “misc prose”. Everything must be typed.

2. Variant Rules (anti-spam law)
Explicitly state:
Which sections require 5–7 variants
Which require ≥3
What “semantic sameness” means (not just wording)
What cannot be restated later in the book
This protects Google Play / Apple at scale.

3. Scene vs Story (this matters)
The spec must hammer this distinction:
SCENE = persona + topic + location moment (you are there)
STORY = named narrative from story bank (governed arc)
Scenes are written by content team. Stories are selected by the planner.
This prevents duplication of narrative function.

4. Location Writing Rules
Content team needs clear instructions like:
Never hardcode cities
Always write scenes assuming {location_class}
Let location affect:
social pressure
pacing
sensory details
norms
Examples:
urban_east_coast
college_town
suburban_midwest
dense_asian_metro

5. Exercise Progression (long-form only)
The spec must define:
Where exercises appear across a long book
When brief vs full is allowed
No re-teaching the same exercise conceptually
Progression, not repetition
This avoids the earlier wrapper chaos.

What NOT to do yet
Do not:
Finalize schema
Implement validators
Write story policies
Build assemblers
All of those are downstream of writing reality.

Phase 5: V4 Variant Selector (Deterministic)
Create: phoenix_v4/assembler/variant_selector.py
What it does:
class VariantSelector:
    """Deterministic variant selection based on book_seed."""
    
    def select_variant(self, section_id: str, book_seed: int, chapter: int, section: int) -> str:
        """
        Select variant deterministically.
        
        Algorithm:
        1. Get all variants for section_id from registry
        2. Use hash(book_seed, chapter, section) to select index
        3. Return variant_id
        
        Same book_seed + same chapter + same section = same variant
        """
        variants = self.section_loader.list_variants_for_section(section_id)
        hash_value = hash((book_seed, chapter, section))
        variant_index = hash_value % len(variants)
        return variants[variant_index]
class VariantSelector:    """Deterministic variant selection based on book_seed."""        def select_variant(self, section_id: str, book_seed: int, chapter: int, section: int) -> str:        """        Select variant deterministically.                Algorithm:        1. Get all variants for section_id from registry        2. Use hash(book_seed, chapter, section) to select index        3. Return variant_id                Same book_seed + same chapter + same section = same variant        """        variants = self.section_loader.list_variants_for_section(section_id)        hash_value = hash((book_seed, chapter, section))        variant_index = hash_value % len(variants)        return variants[variant_index]
Phase 6: V4 Section Assembler
Create: phoenix_v4/assembler/section_assembler.py
What it does:
class SectionAssembler:
    """Assembles individual sections with variant selection."""
    
    def assemble_section(self, section_plan: SectionPlan, book_plan: BookPlanV4) -> str:
        """
        Assemble a single section.
        
        Process:
        1. Select variant deterministically (based on book_seed)
        2. Load variant content
        3. If SCENE type: hydrate location variables
        4. If story-eligible: inject story (if story_plan exists)
        5. Character substitution
        6. Return assembled section content
        """
        # Select variant
        variant_id = self.variant_selector.select_variant(
            section_plan.section_id,
            book_plan.book_seed,
            section_plan.chapter,
            section_plan.section
        )
        
        # Load variant content
        variant = self.section_loader.load_section_variant(variant_id)
        content = variant["content"]
        
        # Location hydration (if location_aware)
        if section_plan.location_aware:
            content = self.location_hydrator.hydrate(
                content,
                section_plan.location_profile
            )
        
        # Story injection (if story-eligible and story_plan exists)
        if section_plan.story_eligible and chapter_plan.story_plan:
            story_content = self.story_loader.load_story_v4(...)
            content = self._inject_story(content, story_content)
        
        # Character substitution
        content = self._substitute_character(content, book_plan.character_id)
        
        return content
class SectionAssembler:    """Assembles individual sections with variant selection."""        def assemble_section(self, section_plan: SectionPlan, book_plan: BookPlanV4) -> str:        """        Assemble a single section.                Process:        1. Select variant deterministically (based on book_seed)        2. Load variant content        3. If SCENE type: hydrate location variables        4. If story-eligible: inject story (if story_plan exists)        5. Character substitution        6. Return assembled section content        """        # Select variant        variant_id = self.variant_selector.select_variant(            section_plan.section_id,            book_plan.book_seed,            section_plan.chapter,            section_plan.section        )                # Load variant content        variant = self.section_loader.load_section_variant(variant_id)        content = variant["content"]                # Location hydration (if location_aware)        if section_plan.location_aware:            content = self.location_hydrator.hydrate(                content,                section_plan.location_profile            )                # Story injection (if story-eligible and story_plan exists)        if section_plan.story_eligible and chapter_plan.story_plan:            story_content = self.story_loader.load_story_v4(...)            content = self._inject_story(content, story_content)                # Character substitution        content = self._substitute_character(content, book_plan.character_id)                return content

         ↓

Key Differences: V3 vs V4 Processing
Aspect	V3	V4
Source	Experience files (exp_XX.md)	Section library (parsed from intake chapters)
Structure	Chapter-first (experience + optional components)	Section-first (8-14 sections per chapter)
Variants	Optional, CSV-based	Mandatory, section-based
Variant Selection	CSV lookup	Deterministic hash-based selection
Location	Light hydration	Location-aware scenes (persona + topic + location_class)
Stories	Optional, limited	Core structural layer, governed
Content Source	Single experience per chapter	Multiple sections with variants per chapter
Implementation Order
Create phoenix_v4/tools/parse_intake_chapters.py (parse & extract)
Run parser on all 12 chapter files (one-time migration)
Create section registry (phoenix_v4/libraries/sections/registry.yaml)
Build SectionLoader (load variants from library)
Build VariantSelector (deterministic selection)
Build SectionAssembler (assemble sections with variants)
Build V4 plan schema (section-first structure)
Build V4 validator (variant coverage enforcement)
Build V4 main assembler (orchestrates section assembly)
Test with example V4 plan
Benefits of This Approach
Preserves canonical content: intake chapters remain the source of truth
Enables variant coverage: each section has multiple variants
Deterministic: same book_seed → same variant selection
Scalable: can generate many books from the same section library
Location-aware: scenes can be location-specific
Governed: stories integrated with strict policy
Section-first: natural fit for long-form books
Should I start by creating the parser tool to extract sections and variants from the intake chapters?

ChatGPT said:
Short answer first, then precise guidance.
Yes — this plan is fundamentally correct. Yes — the first thing dev should do is build the parser.
But there are 3 critical clarifications / constraints you must lock now so this doesn’t drift again.

1. Answer to dev’s question (what you reply)
You should answer dev with this exact response (copy/paste):

Yes — start with the parser. This is a one-time migration step.
Constraints to follow:
Intake chapters are canonical and immutable
Parser must be lossless
No rewriting, normalizing, or “cleaning”
Preserve exact text, paragraphing, punctuation
Parser is mechanical only
Read → extract → serialize
No inference, no heuristics, no guessing
Everything needed is already explicitly in the files
Variant identity must remain stable
variant_id must be deterministic and permanent
Never regenerate IDs differently on reruns
Once the parser output exists, the rest of V4 is straightforward plumbing.
Proceed.

That’s it. No more debate.

2. Why the plan is right (and why this is the only safe order)
Your dev plan succeeds because it respects the correct dependency order:
Correct order (what you finally landed on)
Canonical prose exists first ✅
System extracts structure from prose
Registry becomes the contract
Plans reference registry
Assembler only stitches
The mistake you kept hitting before
You were trying to:
invent structure first
then force prose to conform
This plan reverses that — correctly.

3. Three mandatory constraints to add (important)
These are not optional. Add them to the dev task.
A. Parser must be idempotent
If you run it twice on the same input:
Output must be byte-for-byte identical
Same filenames
Same variant_ids
Same registry order
This guarantees auditability.

B. Registry is read-only after generation
Once registry.yaml is generated:
No code may mutate it
No runtime updates
No auto-fixes
If something is wrong → fix intake content, rerun parser.

C. Variant selection must never see prose
VariantSelector must operate on IDs only, never text.
Why:
Prevents “smart” selection
Prevents accidental semantic clustering
Keeps Google / Apple variance mathematically provable

4. One tiny improvement I recommend (safe, optional)
In variant_fX.yaml, add one extra field:


fingerprint:
  char_count: 923
  paragraph_count: 6
  sentence_count: 14

Why:
Gives you provable diversity metrics
Lets you audit “spam similarity” defensively later
Zero behavioral impact on assembly
This is optional but smart.

5. Reality check (important reassurance)
Nothing in this plan:
Reopens content debates
Requires LLM creativity
Risks rewriting voice
Creates platform risk

Examples:
“You had something to share in .”
“It’s the middle of the day. You’re in , staring at a conversation.”
“There’s a version of you that went to .”
Cause (expected):
Location tokens were stripped ({location.digital_space}, etc.)
No grammatical fallback or sentence repair pass
Impact:
Reader-visible grammar breaks
Breaks immersion
Not acceptable for final retail upload
Fix (simple, deterministic): Add Gate 5: Location Sentence Repair, e.g.:
Regex cleanups: in \. → remove phrase
Or replace with neutral fallback (“online”, “out there”, “somewhere public”)
This is not a creative task. It’s a mechanical cleanup pass.

Only 1 full guided practice per book

ChatGPT said:

Thought for a couple of seconds


Perfect. Here’s a dev-grade simulation/learning loop for 10k+ V4 book builds that will actually teach you what to fix, not just “run tests.”
Goal
Run 10,000 deterministic assemblies across seeds (and optionally personas/locations), then:
measure spam/fingerprint risk
bucket failures into fixable categories (location artifacts, repetition, pacing, etc.)
auto-generate actionable reports
gate releases in CI

1) What to simulate
A. Minimal (fastest, highest signal)
Same plan, vary book_seed: 10k runs
Purpose: validate variant selection, repetition risk, output cleanliness, grammar artifacts.
B. Standard (recommended)
Same plan, vary:
book_seed (10k)
location_profile (e.g., 10 profiles × 1k seeds)
Purpose: catches location hydration + cleanup artifacts.
C. Full matrix (later)
persona × topic × location_profile × seed
Purpose: catalog-scale spam risk modeling.

2) What to measure per book (store as JSONL)
You want metrics that correlate with platform spam heuristics and reader-visible quality.
Cleanliness (hard gates)
unresolved_vars_count (should be 0)
marker_count (should be 0)
double_space_rate
broken_loc_artifacts_count (e.g., “in .”, “at ,”)
dangling_punct_count (, ., in ,, to .)
Similarity / Fingerprint risk
exact_duplicate_paragraphs_across_corpus
exact_duplicate_sentences_across_corpus
top_ngrams_6_plus occurrences
first_sentence_hash per section (collision rate)
section_sequence_signature (e.g., HOOK/SCENE/… pattern is same—fine—but content shouldn’t collide)
Style / Flow health
avg_sentence_len, std_sentence_len
paragraph_count
reading_grade_estimate (optional)
chapter_word_counts distribution
exercise_density (% of words inside practices)
Story health
stories_injected_count
story_id_reuse_within_book (must be 0)
story_id_reuse_across_10k (allowed, but watch frequency)
Store these per run in something like: phoenix_v4/sim/out/runs.jsonl

3) Failure bucketing (this is the “learn” part)
Don’t just “fail”; classify.
Bucket categories (practical)
LOC_ARTIFACT Examples: “in .”, “at ,”, “went to .”
PUNCT_DANGLING “ ,” “ .” “( )” “—” isolated
REPETITION_NGRAM 6+gram repeats across books beyond threshold
PARA_DUPLICATE Exact paragraph collision too often
OPENING_COLLISION Same first 10 words too frequent
EXERCISE_OVERDENSITY Too many long practices / too instructional
STORY_OVERFREQUENCY Same story appears in too many outputs
Each bucket should output:
count
examples (seed + chapter + excerpt)
fix recommendation (which gate/policy/variant set)

4) The key enhancement you already need: “Location Repair Gate”
Right now you’re stripping {location.*} and leaving grammar scars. That’s exactly what 10k sims will surface.
Implement a deterministic Gate 5: Surface Repair, with targeted, testable rewrites:
A. Phrase-level deletions (safe)
r"\b(in|at|on)\s*[.,]" -> ""
r"\b(to|from)\s*[.,]" -> ""
r"\s+,([.!?])" -> r"\1"
r"\s+\.+" -> "."
r"\(\s*\)" -> ""
B. Neutral fallbacks (better than deletion in some slots)
If token removed in a SCENE, replace with neutral anchor:
{location.digital_space} → “online” / “on your phone”
{location.social_gathering} → “a get-together” / “a place with people”
Important: do this in the hydrator before removal, or do a post-pass that detects “in .” and inserts fallback based on section_type=SCENE.
Simulation will tell you which strategy yields fewer artifacts.

5) Corpus-wide spam-risk scoring
After 10k outputs, compute:
A. Collision rates
% books sharing any paragraph hash
% books sharing any 6-gram above K occurrences
% books sharing identical section openings
B. “Spam score” heuristic (simple, effective)
Weighted score per book:
+5 if any LOC_ARTIFACT
+3 if any repeated paragraph seen >N times
+2 if 6-gram collision count high
+1 if opening collisions high
Then rank:
worst 100 books (investigate)
worst 10 sections (add variants)

6) What to fix based on results (typical outcomes)
If LOC_ARTIFACT dominates (likely)
✅ Add Gate 5 + fallback strategy ✅ Add section-level rule: scenes must include a non-token anchor (“on your phone”) so removal still reads fine
If paragraph collisions dominate
✅ Increase variants in high-collision sections (often REFLECTION/INTEGRATION) ✅ Enforce “minimum variant count” for ALL sections, not just HOOK/SCENE
If openings collide
✅ Add “first sentence diversity” validator across variant libraries
If stories over-repeat
✅ Add story-frequency cap per simulation batch ✅ Expand story bank tiers, or broaden adjacent-topic pool

7) CI integration (so this doesn’t regress)
Add 3 layers:
A. PR quick gate (fast)
50 seeds
must be 0 hard-gate fails
LOC_ARTIFACT must be 0
B. Nightly medium
1,000 seeds
produce report + trend graphs
C. Weekly heavy
10,000 seeds
compute collision rates + spam score distribution
store artifacts for regression comparison

8) What to tell dev (short + actionable)

ChatGPT said:
Got it — here are (1) hard numeric safety thresholds, (2) exactly what sections to expand, and (3) a minimal-effort variant expansion playbook that your content team + dev can run like a factory.

1) Hard numeric thresholds for “not spam” safety (practical, testable)
These aren’t “Google/Apple official” (they don’t publish exact numbers). These are engineering thresholds that correlate strongly with “this won’t look mass-duplicated” when you do large-scale generation.
A. Book-level similarity gates (10,000-run simulation)
Gate A1 — Duplicate book rate
Target: 0 exact duplicates out of 10,000
Fail: ≥ 1 exact duplicate
Gate A2 — Near-duplicate rate (fingerprint similarity) Define a simple similarity score per pair:
Compare paragraph-hash sets (or shingled paragraph hashes)
Flag a “near-duplicate” if ≥ 80% paragraphs match
Target: ≤ 0.05% of books have a near-duplicate (≤ 5 / 10,000)
Warn: 0.05%–0.2%
Fail: > 0.2%
Gate A3 — Chapter-level identicalness
Target: For each chapter number (1..12), the % of runs that produce the exact same chapter text should be:
HOOK/SCENE/INTEGRATION sections present: ≤ 2%
Otherwise (low-variance chapters): ≤ 5%
Fail: any chapter ≥ 10%
Why: even if whole books aren’t identical, a marketplace can still see “mass-reused chapter text”.

B. Component collision gates (what your current report is hinting at)
You should track collisions by section_id and by section_type.
Gate B1 — Unique paragraph hashes Measure: unique_paragraph_hashes / total_unique_paragraphs_possible In practice, just use a per-run aggregate:
Target: ≥ 2,000 unique paragraph hashes across 10k runs (for a 12-chapter book with your structure)
Warn: 1,000–2,000
Fail: < 1,000
(Your current 1k-run shows 234 unique paragraph hashes, which is fine for early-stage + limited variants, but it won’t scale safely unless you expand the right sections.)
Gate B2 — “Hot paragraph” dominance Count the most common paragraph hash frequency.
Target: top paragraph appears in ≤ 0.5% of books (≤ 50 / 10,000)
Warn: 0.5%–1%
Fail: > 1%
Gate B3 — 6-gram reuse You already track “6-gram collisions above 10 occurrences.” Tighten it:
Target: #(6-grams occurring > 50 times) = 0
Warn: 1–20
Fail: > 20
This prevents repeated “signature phrases” that make outputs look templated.

C. Story diversity gates (must fix instrumentation first)
Once story IDs are tracked:
Gate C1 — Unique story IDs
Target: ≥ 250 unique story IDs used across 10k runs (assuming your bank is large)
Warn: 100–250
Fail: < 100
Gate C2 — Hot story dominance
Target: top story used ≤ 0.5% of books
Fail: > 1%

2) Exactly which sections to expand (highest ROI)
You do not need to expand everything equally. Expand the sections that create most collisions.
Priority order (do these first)
Tier 1: Must expand (highest platform risk)
INTEGRATION (book framing, “what this is / isn’t”, closing invitation)
REFLECTION / TEACHING (pattern explanation, core reframe, thesis preview)
HOOK (already has variants — keep strong)
Why: These are the sections that tend to repeat across books and scream “templated self-help”.
Tier 2: Expand next (good ROI, lower risk)
SCENE (already good; expand by location + persona context richness)
BRIDGES / TRANSITIONS (tiny but powerful collision reducers)
Tier 3: Optional / later
Micro-copy blocks (one-liners, “pause” sentences, rhetorical questions)

3) Variant Expansion Playbook (minimal effort, maximal uniqueness)
This is how your content team should write variants so you get real uniqueness without ballooning workload.
Step 0 — Dev produces a “Collision Heatmap”
From 1k or 10k runs, output:
Top 25 section_ids by:
paragraph reuse
sentence reuse
6-gram hotspots
Top repeated phrases (6–10 grams)
“First sentence” collisions per section_id
This tells content exactly what to rewrite first.

Step 1 — Upgrade variant policy (what content must deliver)
Required variant counts (per section type):
HOOK: 7 variants
SCENE: 7 variants
REFLECTION / TEACHING: 5 variants (currently your weak point)
INTEGRATION: 7 variants (highest risk)
BRIDGE / TRANSITION: 9 micro-variants (short)
If your workload is tight: Do INTEGRATION + REFLECTION first. That alone usually collapses collision rates.

Step 2 — Variant Families (F1–F5) must apply to every section, not just scenes/hooks
Right now you’re treating F1–F5 as mainly HOOK/SCENE. For platform safety, do it for REFLECTION/INTEGRATION too.
Use this mapping:
F1 Sensory/Body: “Here’s what it feels like…”
F2 Dialogue/Inner voice: “You tell yourself…”
F3 Context/Mechanism: “Here’s the loop…”
F4 Imagery/Metaphor: “It’s like…”
F5 Consequence/Cost: “And the cost is…”
Rule: same meaning, different cognitive entry point.

Step 3 — “Anchor + Swap” method (fast writing, big uniqueness)
Instead of rewriting whole sections from scratch:
Each section variant is built from:
Anchor sentences (2–3 sentences that preserve the chapter’s truth)
Swap blocks (3–6 sentences that change)
Example for REFLECTION:
Keep 2 anchor lines: the core reframe
Swap: mechanism explanation, examples, metaphors, cadence
This yields fast variants without losing voice.

Step 4 — Micro-variants (the cheapest collision killer)
For each chapter, create a micro-variant bank:
Openers (10)
Closers (10)
Bridge lines (15)
Rhetorical questions (15)
Then each REFLECTION / INTEGRATION section includes:
{micro.opener}
{micro.bridge}
{micro.closer}
These are short, but they shatter paragraph hashing collisions.
If you don’t want variables: treat them as append-only inserts chosen deterministically.

Step 5 — “Do not repeat” rules (content team checklist)
Hard rules that reduce platform fingerprints:
No repeated first 12 words across variants in the same section_id
No repeated last sentence across variants in the same section_id
Banlist any phrase that appears too often (generated by dev report)
Avoid stable “template tells” like:
“Here’s the thing—”
“Let me be clear—”
“What nobody tells you—” (Use them, but rotate aggressively.)

What to tell Dev (simple + exact)

collision heatmap by section_id

INTEGRATION + REFLECTION/TEACHING
then bridges

HOOK 7, SCENE 7, REFLECTION 5, INTEGRATION 7, BRIDGE 9

Below is a clear diagnosis, what is actually happening, and precise fixes that will survive 10k+ runs and platform scrutiny.

1. What the data is telling you (plain English)
Observation
All 964 runs used the same story ID gen_z_nyc_anxiety_en_backfill_89e57bb06c Tier: T1 (persona-specific + exact topic)
Why this is happening (by design)
Your story injector is working correctly:
The plan has exactly one story slot
The story bank has only one eligible story at Tier 1
Tier 1 has the highest weight (2.0)
Deterministic selection + highest-weight-only candidate = same story every time
So the system is saying:
“If I am Gen Z + Anxiety + NYC and there is exactly one perfect match, always pick it.”
That is correct behavior, but unsafe at scale.

2. This is a platform risk — but a fixable one
Why this will be flagged by Google Play / Apple
Even if the rest of the book varies:
Same story paragraph(s)
Same emotional beat
Same named character + setting
Across hundreds or thousands of books, this looks like:
“Mass-produced derivative content with reused narrative blocks”
This is exactly the kind of thing stores quietly downrank.

3. Do NOT fix this by randomization ❌
Important: do not add randomness. That would break:
Determinism
Reproducibility
CI validation
Debuggability
We fix this structurally, not probabilistically.

4. The correct fix: Story Coverage + Tier Softening
You need three coordinated changes.

FIX 1 — Enforce minimum story coverage per slot
Add a hard gate in simulation + CI:
New Story Coverage Gate
For every story slot defined in a plan:


story_slot:
  min_unique_story_ids: 5

CI rule:
If unique_story_ids < min_unique_story_ids
❌ FAIL simulation
This forces story banks to be populated before scale.
📌 For Gen Z + Anxiety + NYC: You need at least 5 persona-appropriate stories for that slot.

FIX 2 — Add “Tier softening” after N uses (still deterministic)
Right now Tier 1 is absolute. That’s the real issue.
New rule: Tier Saturation Softening
Conceptually:
“Perfect matches are preferred — but not endlessly.”
Deterministic softening logic
Add a story usage counter keyed by (persona, topic, layer) during simulation:


if story_usage_count[story_id] >= SATURATION_THRESHOLD:
    reduce_weight(story_id)

Suggested values:
SATURATION_THRESHOLD = 50 (per 10k run)
After threshold:
Tier 1 weight drops from 2.0 → 1.0
Tier 2 remains at 1.5
Tier 3 remains at 1.0
Result:
Early runs still favor Tier 1
Later runs naturally spread across Tier 2 / Tier 3
Still deterministic (depends on run index + seed)
This mimics editorial diversity, not randomness.

FIX 3 — Split “story” into story families (low effort, huge gain)
You do not need 100 totally new stories.
Instead, create story families:


story_family_id: genz_nyc_anxiety_classroom
variants:
  - id: genz_nyc_anxiety_classroom_v1
  - id: genz_nyc_anxiety_classroom_v2
  - id: genz_nyc_anxiety_classroom_v3

Rules:
Same emotional truth
Different:
opening sentence
sensory details
internal dialogue
resolution beat
The injector treats each variant as a unique story ID.
📌 This is the highest ROI fix. 3–5 variants per family is enough.

5. What you should expect after fixes (targets)
After implementing the above and rerunning 10k sims:
Story Health Targets
Metric
Target
Unique story IDs
≥ 20
Top story usage
≤ 1%
Avg stories/book
unchanged
Tier distribution
T1 dominant early, mixed later
